items.title: Copy Less, Ground More: Overcoming Repetitive Copying in Long-Context Reasoning via Evidence-Aware Reinforcement Learning
items.authors: Lizhe Fang, Weizhou Shen, Tianyi Tang et al.
items.abstract: Large language models that generate step-by-step reasoning traces have achieved strong performance on complex tasks, and extending them to long-context settings has emerged as an important frontier. However, we identify a critical failure mode in this regime: \emph{repetitive copying}, where models ...

items.title: Appearance Pointers -- Multimodal Region Control of Diffusion Transformers
items.authors: Rahul Sajnani, Yulia Gryaditskaya, Radomír Měch et al.
items.abstract: Controllable image generation remains challenging for creative professionals, who often require precise regional control over materials, object identities, and spatial arrangements that cannot be reliably achieved through text prompting alone. Diffusion Transformers (DiTs) can natively ingest hetero...

items.title: Agents in the Wild: Where Research Meets Deployment
items.authors: Grace Hui Yang, Pranav N. Venkit, Hooman Sedghamiz et al.
items.abstract: Agentic systems large language model (LLM) based architectures capable of reasoning, planning, acting, and coordinating with tools and other agents are rapidly transitioning from research prototypes to production scale deployments across domains such as software engineering, scientific discovery, an...

items.title: Masked Visual Actions for Unified World Modeling
items.authors: Hadi Alzayer, Wenlong Huang, Haonan Chen et al.
items.abstract: Video models absorb rich priors over how the visual world moves, interacts, and responds to contact, making them promising substrates for robotic world modeling. The central challenge is how to communicate action to such models in a form aligned with the visual space in which they learned these inte...

items.title: 1-Lipschitz Neural Networks on Hadamard Manifolds
items.authors: Davide Murari, Marta Ghirardelli, Ben Adcock et al.
items.abstract: Controlling the Lipschitz constant of a neural network is a standard way to promote robustness and stability. Most existing constraining strategies are designed for Euclidean spaces. In this work, we construct and analyze a class of 1-Lipschitz neural networks on Hadamard manifolds. Our layers are o...

items.title: Fundamental limits of distributed multiclass classification from simple binary decisions
items.authors: Ioannis Papageorgiou, Srinivas Nomula, Ayalvadi Ganesh et al.
items.abstract: We consider the problem of constructing a $K$-class classifier from the combination of $O(\log K)$ simple binary classifiers -- this is a natural paradigm to construct a sophisticated classifier in a distributed manner with each agent performing a relatively straightforward task. We study the fundam...

items.title: From Distances to Trajectories: Real-Time Signed Distance Function Mapping and Distance-Accelerated Motion Planning for UAVs
items.authors: Jason Stanley, Zhirui Dai, Qihao Qian et al.
items.abstract: Autonomous flight in cluttered environments requires a robot to build a geometric map of its surroundings and plan safe, dynamically feasible trajectories, all onboard and in real time. Conventional approaches treat mapping and planning as separate stages and often rely on binary occupancy for colli...
